description_long: This object is a roughly hewn, dark gray stone with an irregular, somewhat flattened oval shape. Its surface is uneven, featuring a mix of smooth and jagged areas, with visible natural fractures and subtle color variations ranging from deep gray to brownish hues. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings, and has a slightly glossy sheen in some spots, likely due to weathering or partial polishing. One side of the stone displays a more pronounced, angular edge, while the opposite side is broader and flatter, suggesting it may have been shaped or worn down over time. There are no artificial carvings or markings, but some areas show faint mottling and natural mineral veining. This stone could easily be found as a standalone obstacle or barrier in an urban park, garden, or along a landscaped pathway.
description: A dense, irregularly shaped dark gray stone with a mix of smooth and jagged surfaces, showing natural fractures and subtle color variations. Its broad, flat side and angular edge suggest weathering or shaping over time.
description_view_0: The front view shows the broadest, flattest face of the stone, highlighting its uneven surface and mottled gray-brown coloration.
description_view_1: The side view reveals the stone's thin profile and pronounced angular edge, emphasizing its irregular, wedge-like shape.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a slightly more rounded and less textured surface, with subtle mineral veining and a lighter gray tone.
description_view_3: The opposite side view shows the stone's thickness and a small label, with a mix of smooth and rough textures along the edge.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle natural mottling and mineral veining, irregular and organic in appearance
attribute_weathering: Partially weathered with a mix of smooth and rough areas, some glossy spots from exposure
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with manicured lawns or paved surfaces, but can blend with other natural stones or vegetation in landscaped settings
